summary: AI stocks Vertiv, Lumentum and Coherent, all with strong Nvidia ties, will join the S&P 500 before the open on March 23, along with EchoStar.

headline: Vertiv and EchoStar lead tech surge in S&P 500 rebalance
summary: Investing.com -- S&P Dow Jones Indices announced a sweeping overhaul of its benchmark tracking on Friday, propelling several technology and industrial firms into the prestigious S&P 500. Shares of Vertiv Holdings Co (NYSE:VRT) surged 5.6% in after-hours trading following the news, while EchoStar Corporation (NASDAQ:SATS) and Lumentum Holdings Inc (NASDAQ:LITE) also saw significant gains. The quarterly reshuffle is designed to maintain the integrity of the market’s most-watched gauges. S&P DJI st

headline: MU, LRCX, AMAT, GEV All Added to the S&P 100 As AI Stocks Gain Influence

headline: How Micron’s New 256GB AI Server Memory and India Ramp-Up Will Impact Micron Technology (MU) Investors
summary: In early March 2026, Micron Technology began shipping customer samples of its 256GB SOCAMM2 LPDDR5X module, the highest-capacity low‑power DRAM for AI data center servers, while also ramping a major new assembly and test facility in Sanand, India that converts DRAM and NAND wafers into finished products. Together, these advances highlight Micron’s push to pair cutting‑edge, power‑efficient AI memory architectures with a broader global manufacturing footprint aimed at supporting future demand...

Together, these advances highlight Micron’s push to pair cutting‑edge, power‑efficient AI memory architectures with a broader global manufacturing footprint aimed at supporting future demand...

headline: Micron's Setup Sweetens Despite The Current Market Meltdown
summary: Micron is approaching its 50-day moving average amid the recent market meltdown and could offer a new buy point if the stock rebounds from that level.  After rocketing 239% in 2025, Micron stock has gained 39% thus far in 2026, as of Thursday.  It holds the top spot in Investor's Business Daily's IBD 50 list of growth stocks.

headline: Oil Surges 4%, Reviving Inflation Fears That Sent Wall Street Sliding
summary: A resurgent oil price sparked fresh inflation anxiety on Thursday, pushing the Dow Jones down as much as 1,100 points intraday. Rate-cut expectations were slashed, chip stocks broadly retreated, though Broadcom's audacious $100 billion AI forecast and The Trade Desk's OpenAI rumor provided rare pockets of green.
